title: notifications core — health probes
description: Probes registered with foundation's HealthAggregator at boot. Core probes are aggregate-substrate checks; per-provider reachability probes live in the respective channel module (notifications-mail, notifications-sms, notifications-push, notifications-in-app).

probes.name: notifications-category-registry-loaded
probes.description: The category registry loaded on ApplicationBooted — every module's notifications.json was scanned + cached. Fails at boot only. Critical because a missing category = failed dispatch.

probes.name: notifications-template-registry-loaded
probes.description: The template registry cache is warm + non-empty. Fails when the seed hasn't run or when the cache was invalidated + not yet refreshed. Critical because template lookup is on the hot path.

probes.name: notifications-deliveries-oldest-pending-age
probes.description: Age of the oldest NotificationDelivery in state=queued or state=sending. Warns > 5m; fails > 30m. Non-critical — backlog doesn't stop new dispatches, but sustained backlog indicates worker starvation.

probes.name: notifications-digest-worker-running
probes.description: The notifications-digests Horizon queue has at least one active supervisor. Fails when digests aren't being processed. Non-critical — digests can catch up on the next scheduled run.

probes.name: notifications-preference-cache-fresh
probes.description: Preference cache hit ratio (rolling 5m). Warns < 90%; fails < 50%. Non-critical — low hit ratio degrades performance but not correctness.

probes.name: notifications-dispatch-kill-switch
probes.description: Reports whether the master dispatch kill switch is OFF (i.e. dispatches suppressed). Non-critical: it's an ops signal, not a fault. Fails-open so operators can page during a dispatch-off incident.
